chunks.text: CS Distinguished Alumni Award Winners - EECS at Berkeley CS Distinguished Alumni Award Winners Distinguished Alumni Awards winners are selected each year by the EE and CS Chairs in consultation with the EECS Faculty Awards Committee and with input from the EECS faculty. Winners receive a plaque awarded at a departmental event such as the BEARS conference or the L&S CS Commencement. 2025 Dana Angluin (Ph.D. 1976, advisor: Manuel Blum), Professor Emeritus of CS, Yale John Schulman (Ph.D. 2016, advisor: Pieter Abbeel), Co-Founder of OpenAI and creator of ChatGPT 2024 Nancy Amato (M.S. 1988, advisor: Manuel Blum), Abel Bliss Professor and CS Department Head, UIUC Lars Rasmussen (Ph.D. 1998, advisor: Alistair Sinclair), Angel Investor, co-founder of Google Maps, Google Wave, and Weav Music 2023 Paul Debevec (Ph.D. 1996, advisor: Jitendra Malik), Director of Production Innovative Research, Netflix; Adjunct Research Professor, USC Computer Science Andrea and Remzi Arpaci-Dusseau Andrea Arpaci-Dusseau (M.S. 1994/Ph.D. 1998, adviser: David Culler), Professor, University of Wisconsin, Madison Remzi Arpaci-Dusseau (M.S. 1996/Ph.D. 1999, adviser David Patterson), Professor, University of Wisconsin, Madison 2022 Kimberly Keeton (MS 1994/PhD 1999, adviser: David Patterson), Principal Engineer, Google Noam Nisan (PhD 1988, advisor: Richard Karp), Professor of Computer Science, Hebrew University of Jerusalem 2021 Ralph Merkle (BA 1974/MS 1977), Senior Research Fellow, Institute for Molecular Manufacturing Hari Balakrishnan (PhD 1998, advisor: Randy Katz), Fujitsu Professor of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence at MIT, Founder/CTO of Cambridge Mobile Telematics 2020 Dan Jurafsky (PhD 1992, advisor: Robert Wilensky), Professor of Linguistics and Computer Science, Stanford
